chunk_summary: This section outlines an age-staged feeding prescription template for infants and young children under 2 years, emphasizing standardized counseling during routine clinic visits to improve feeding practices and measurable health outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of age-staged feeding prescriptions for infants and young children under 2 years.
Learn the five-part format for documenting feeding plans, including milk feeding, complementary foods, texture progression, supplementation, and follow-up.
Recognize the role of consistent counseling in preventing anemia and promoting weight gain and dietary diversity.
key_facts: Feeding prescriptions should specify what to feed, how often, how much, and contingency plans for feeding issues.
Example for a 7-month-old: breastfeed on demand, provide two thick meals daily, enrich meals with ghee/oil, and include iron-rich items like egg/meat or pulses with vitamin C fruit.
Supplementation guidelines: Vitamin D 400 IU daily, iron as per protocol, and deworming from 12 months per local program.
Follow-up plans include weight reviews, 24-hour diet recalls, and linking families to ICDS or ASHA support.
Texture progression: Start with thick puree, advance to mashed, chopped family foods, and safe finger foods.
reference_quote: A practical way to standardize counseling is to write an age-staged 'feeding prescription' at every contact (birth, immunization visits, sick visits, and growth monitoring).
